mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress forward earnings multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance against rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that force equity risk premiums higher and drive negative forward market returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in 70 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, invalidating the threshold effect.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals robust consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: During supply-constrained environments, rising industrial production indicates capacity utilization improvements that boost corporate margins and generate positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces central banks to tighten policy, creating a persistent negative pressure on equity valuations.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes produce a positive Rank IC over 3-year rolling windows, contradicting the inflation-aversion mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth crosses below 1.5 percent, aggregate demand weakness signals impending earnings downgrades that trigger negative equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets deliver positive 60-day returns in 65 percent of quarters where annualized GDP growth drops below 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with rising federal funds rates indicates a tightening labor market that outpaces monetary restraint, supporting equity valuations through earnings growth.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and FEDFUNDS changes yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample regressions with p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In high-inflation regimes, declining housing starts reflect credit market stress and reduced consumer leverage capacity, forecasting negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative HOUST changes fail to predict negative 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 8.0 percent, showing a breakdown in the credit-stress transmission channel.
